context: Tests whether the agent implements background jobs following the rails-background-jobs skill: passing IDs not objects, adding idempotency checks before side effects, using correct retry/discard strategies, keeping perform thin, and configuring the recurring schedule in recurring.yml.

checklist.name: Pass ID not object
checklist.description: SendInvoiceReminderJob.perform_later is called with an invoice ID (integer/string), NOT an Invoice object

checklist.name: Load record in perform
checklist.description: The job's perform method looks up the invoice via a finder (e.g. Invoice.find) using the passed ID, not from an instance variable or argument object

checklist.name: Idempotency check present
checklist.description: SendInvoiceReminderJob checks whether the reminder was already sent (e.g. checks reminder_sent_at or a flag) BEFORE sending the email

checklist.name: Side effect after guard
checklist.description: The mailer call (InvoiceMailer or similar) appears AFTER the idempotency guard, not before it

checklist.name: retry_on with attempts
checklist.description: retry_on is used for at least one transient error class AND includes an explicit attempts: N limit (not open-ended)

checklist.name: discard_on permanent error
checklist.description: discard_on is used for at least one permanent/non-retriable error (e.g. ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound)

checklist.name: Thin perform — service delegation
checklist.description: SendInvoiceReminderJob delegates business logic to a service object or mailer rather than containing multi-step orchestration inline in perform

checklist.name: Recurring in recurring.yml
checklist.description: config/recurring.yml exists and defines the nightly sweep job with a cron or schedule expression targeting 08:00 UTC

checklist.name: Solid Queue adapter
checklist.description: The implementation uses Solid Queue as the queue adapter (referenced in config, gemfile, or process_log.md) — not Sidekiq or async

checklist.name: Spec covers idempotency
checklist.description: SendInvoiceReminderJob spec includes a test case verifying that enqueueing the job twice does NOT send the email twice (second enqueue is a no-op)

checklist.name: Spec written for job
checklist.description: Both spec files exist (send_invoice_reminder_job_spec.rb and nightly_overdue_invoice_sweep_job_spec.rb) with at least one describe/it block each
